assertion_results.text: The plan explicitly references reusing common/src/db/query.rs::apply_filter for comma-separated multi-value query parameter parsing rather than writing new parsing logic (constraint 5.4)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lines 25-28: 'Use `common/src/db/query.rs::apply_filter` to parse the comma-separated string into individual values and generate the appropriate SQL `IN` clause condition. Do NOT write a custom comma-splitting or filter-building function -- `apply_filter` already handles both single and multi-value cases.' Also reiterated in Code Reuse Strategy (lines 105-108) and reuse-analysis.md Reuse Candidate 1 section (lines 6-29).

assertion_results.text: The plan references following the filter pattern from modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/list.rs as a structural guide for implementing the license filter
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lines 18-22: 'Following the pattern in `modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/list.rs`, add an optional `license: Option<String>` field to the existing query parameter struct used by the list handler. Also in Code Reuse Strategy lines 110-113 and reuse-analysis.md Reuse Candidate 2 section (lines 31-59).

assertion_results.text: The plan references using entity/src/package_license.rs for the JOIN query rather than writing raw SQL or creating a new entity
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lines 30-35: 'Use the SeaORM entity defined in `entity/src/package_license.rs` to construct a JOIN from the `package` table to the `package_license` table... Also in Code Reuse Strategy lines 115-117 and reuse-analysis.md Reuse Candidate 3 section (lines 61-88).

assertion_results.text: The reuse analysis covers all 3 Reuse Candidates from the task description, explaining how each would be applied
assertion_results.evidence: reuse-analysis.md contains three dedicated sections: 'Reuse Candidate 1: common/src/db/query.rs::apply_filter' (lines 6-29), 'Reuse Candidate 2: modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/list.rs' (lines 31-59), 'Reuse Candidate 3: entity/src/package_license.rs' (lines 61-88). Each includes 'What it provides', 'How it would be used', 'Why reuse over reimplementation', and 'Integration point' subsections explaining application in detail.

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections (constraint 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: Task description (task-with-reuse.md) specifies Files to Modify: modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs, modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs; Files to Create: tests/api/package_license_filter.rs. The plan lists exactly these same files: 'Files to Modify' section lists list.rs (line 11) and service/mod.rs (line 44); 'Files to Create' section lists tests/api/package_license_filter.rs (line 66).
